close_reason: Fixed: documented MAX_NV_BUFFER_SIZE limitation in module docs
description: nv_read is called with a single (data_size, offset=0) pair. The TPM 2.0 spec limits each NV_Read to at most MAX_NV_BUFFER_SIZE bytes (typically 1024 on most TPMs, implementation-defined). If data_size > MAX_NV_BUFFER_SIZE, the TPM returns TPM_RC_NV_RANGE or TPM_RC_VALUE, which map_tpm_error surfaces as a Backend error with no explanation. The tss-esapi abstraction layer has nv::read_full() which performs chunked reads. The current code should either use the chunked read helper or document the maximum supported NV size. For NV indices smaller than MAX_NV_BUFFER_SIZE (common case for secrets), this works fine. The practical limit varies by TPM: Intel PTT typically 2048, Infineon SLB 9670 typically 1024.
title: NV read single-shot; large indices silently truncated
